skill.name: pr-review
skill.description: Reviews MiniMax Skills pull requests for structure, frontmatter, secret patterns, documentation quality, and repository checklist compliance.
skill.summary: Review MiniMax Skills pull requests with validation and content quality checks.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: AI review dismisses most static high-risk findings as false positives from Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and regex literals. No prompt injection, network access, command injection, or malicious intent was found. The validator recursively reads local files and prints matched secret fragments. Publication should warn reviewers to limit scope and handle output carefully.

medium_findings.title: Validator Prints Matched Secret Fragments
medium_findings.description: The validation script scans files for high-confidence secret patterns and includes the matched text in error output. This supports review, but it can expose partial secrets into logs or AI conversation context if run on sensitive repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly reads file contents and appends a truncated match to validation errors. This is legitimate security tooling, but the exposure path is clear.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected Recursive Filesystem Scan
medium_findings.description: The validator accepts a path argument, resolves it to an absolute path, and recursively searches for skill directories. This is expected for a validation tool, but reviewers should avoid broad paths that include unrelated private files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Recursive scanning is explicit and path selection is user-controlled. No exfiltration was found, so the risk depends on how the command is run.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives In Documentation And Regexes
low_findings.description: The reported weak crypto, environment access, and backtick execution patterns are mostly Markdown examples, inline code formatting, or regex strings. They do not show command injection, secret use, or malicious execution in context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts are documentation text or static regex definitions. I found no dynamic shell execution, cryptographic operation, or environment variable read at these locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Local File Reading
dangerous_patterns.description: The script walks selected directories and reads matching text files. This is necessary for validation but should be limited to the intended repository path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is directly implemented with os.walk and open. It has a clear legitimate purpose and no network sink.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Command Invocation Requested By Skill
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs reviewers to run a local Python validation script. The command is fixed, but it still executes repository-provided code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible and not attacker-parameterized in the skill text. The concern is normal script execution trust for community skills.

content.value_statement: Skill repository maintainers need consistent checks before merging community submissions. This skill combines automated validation with manual review guidance for structure, secrets, and documentation quality.
actual_capabilities: Guides reviewers through automated validation before manual review.
Checks each skill directory for a required SKILL.md file.
Validates top-level frontmatter fields, including name and description.
Flags common hardcoded secret patterns in supported text files.
Provides quality criteria for descriptions, scope, scripts, and README updates.
Separates blocking errors from warnings that need reviewer attention.
limitations: It is tailored to the MiniMax Skills repository layout.
The validator uses a minimal frontmatter parser, not a full YAML parser.
Secret scanning covers only selected high-confidence patterns.
It does not prove that submitted skill behavior is safe at runtime.

use_cases.title: Review a New Skill Submission
use_cases.description: Run structure checks, then review scope, descriptions, scripts, and README updates before approval.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Check a skill locally before requesting review, and fix blockers before submission.

use_cases.title: Screen for Secret Exposure
use_cases.description: Identify common hardcoded credential patterns and flag risky examples for cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pr-review skill to review this skill pull request. Run validation first, then summarize blockers and warnings.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Skill Directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Contributor preflight before opening a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-review on my skill directory. Check required files, frontmatter, directory naming, and hardcoded secret patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Content Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Manual review after automated checks pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this submitted skill for scope clarity, trigger description quality, file size, script quality, language, and README sync.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Merge Recommendation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintainer review with final decision support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this pull request with pr-review. Separate blocking errors, nonblocking warnings, content suggestions, and your merge recommendation.

output_examples.input: A new skill submission has a missing license, a weak description, and no README entry.
output: Blocking result: no hard validation blocker if required fields pass.
Warnings: add a license, improve trigger wording, and update README tables.

output_examples.input: A submitted skill includes an example token that matches a secret pattern.
output: Blocking result: hardcoded secret pattern detected.
Required fix: remove the token and replace it with a clearly fake placeholder.
Recommendation: do not merge until validation passes.

output_examples.input: A maintainer asks whether a skill overlaps with an existing capability.
output: Review focus: compare the stated scope with existing skills.
Expected evidence: explain the boundary or recommend extending an existing skill.
Recommendation: approve only when the new scope is distinct.

best_practices: Run automated validation before making manual review comments.
Limit validation to the intended repository or skill directory.
Separate merge blockers from suggestions in the final review.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real secret values into review comments or chat.
Do not approve a skill with vague activation triggers.
Do not run the validator on broad personal directories.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews MiniMax Skills pull requests for structure, frontmatter, secrets, content quality, scripts, and README updates.

faq.question: Does it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It runs hard checks and provides guidance, but maintainers still decide whether a submission is appropriate.

faq.question: Can it find every secret?
faq.answer: No. It checks selected high-confidence patterns and asks reviewers to inspect other credential handling manually.

faq.question: Can contributors use it before opening a pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. Contributors can use it to find blockers and warnings before requesting maintainer review.

faq.question: Is it specific to one repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The rules and documentation target the MiniMax Skills repository structure and review process.

faq.question: What should reviewers do with warnings?
faq.answer: Warnings are not automatic blockers, but reviewers should flag them and request improvements when they affect quality.
